name: court_transcript_ai_dialect_error
premise: Judge Camila Reyes must decide whether to pause arraignments after learning that the court's AI transcription system is mistranscribing certain dialects in fast speech. The system has reduced transcript backlog by 60% and allows public defenders to receive records the same day instead of waiting weeks. A court clerk finds that in bail hearings with noisy audio, the system sometimes reverses negations such as 'I did not have the weapon,' especially for speakers with regional dialects. Human stenographers are unavailable until Monday, and delaying arraignments means some defendants will remain detained over the weekend. Continuing with AI transcripts keeps cases moving but risks creating inaccurate records in hearings that affect liberty, bail, and plea negotiations.

agents.name: Judge Camila Reyes
agents.identity: I am the supervising criminal court judge responsible for timely hearings and fair process.
agents.goal: I want defendants heard promptly without relying on records that may distort what they actually said.

agents.name: Malik Turner
agents.identity: I am the senior public defender whose clients are most affected by delayed arraignments and inaccurate transcripts.
agents.goal: I want my clients released or heard quickly, but not at the cost of records that could later harm their cases.

choices: I will pause affected arraignments until human transcription is available, accepting that some defendants remain detained longer.
I will continue hearings using AI transcripts with judicial review of disputed lines, accepting that some errors may not be noticed in time.
forced_choice.call_to_action: Judge Reyes must decide whether to delay liberty hearings or continue using a transcript system with a known dialect-related error. What does Judge Camila Reyes do?
